evals.name: fleet-inventory
evals.prompt: What RBAC do we have across the fleet? Give me a per-cluster breakdown.
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-rbac preflight` first, then `cub-rbac snapshot` (JSON, or -o table) and reports the per-cluster counts of roles/cluster-roles/role-bindings/cluster-role-bindings/service-accounts plus totals. Does not mutate, does not fall back to kubectl.

evals.name: list-clusterrolebindings-one-cluster
evals.prompt: List every ClusterRoleBinding on the prod-use2-oci cluster.
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-rbac list --kind ClusterRoleBinding --cluster prod-use2-oci` (optionally -o table or piped through jq for names) and reports the bindings. Uses the --kind and --cluster filters rather than post-filtering, does not mutate.

evals.name: service-accounts-by-cluster
evals.prompt: How many service accounts does each cluster have?
evals.expected_output: Uses `cub-rbac snapshot` and reads the serviceAccounts count per cluster (e.g. via jq on the JSON), reporting the per-cluster numbers. Does not enumerate via kubectl.

evals.name: routes-effective-access-elsewhere
evals.prompt: Who can read secrets in our prod clusters?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is an effective-access question, not inventory, and routes to the rbac-whocan skill / `cub-rbac who-can get secrets` rather than answering with `cub-rbac list`.
